_comment: Template for deployment config. Copy to dev.json / prod.json and fill in the values. Fields marked OPTIONAL can be omitted — defaults are shown.

deploymentConfig._comment_deploymentIdentity: Choose ONE auth option below. Rename it to 'deploymentIdentity' and remove the other two.

deploymentIdentity_option1_azure_sp._comment: Azure-registered service principal — recommended for CI/CD (GitHub Actions)

deploymentIdentity_option2_oauth_m2m._comment: Databricks-native service principal via OAuth M2M

deploymentIdentity_option3_pat._comment: Personal access token — simplest for ad-hoc local testing

deploymentConfig._comment_jobRunIdentity: SP or user identity for the Databricks job. clientId is used as (1) Unity Catalog schema name in the volume path and (2) run_as in production mode. When absent: volume schema = 'default', run_as is omitted.
deploymentConfig._comment_volumeCatalog: Unity Catalog catalog for job resource volumes. Default: ctdp<stage>dbxlakehouse (e.g. ctdpdevdbxlakehouse, ctdpproddbxlakehouse).
deploymentConfig._comment_targetMode: 'production' (default) emits run_as using jobRunIdentity. 'development' omits run_as so the job runs as the deploying user.
deploymentConfig._comment_clusterConfiguration: All sub-fields are optional with defaults. The entire block can be omitted.

clusterConfiguration._comment_clusterPolicyId: Cluster policy ID. Omit to create clusters without a policy.
clusterConfiguration._comment_nodeTypes: Worker and driver VM sizes. Default: Standard_D8ds_v5. Ignored when instancePoolId is set.
clusterConfiguration._comment_instancePoolId: Azure instance pool ID. When set, cluster node types are governed by the pool. Exposed as bundle variable 'instance_pool_id' for included resource files.

deploymentConfig._comment_resourceFilesPath: Path relative to project root to a resource YAML file or directory (*.yml / *.yaml). Files are included in the bundle via 'include:'. Omit to deploy without extra resource files.
deploymentConfig._comment_schedule: Cron schedule for the job. Omit to leave the job unscheduled (trigger manually or via API).

schedule._comment_quartzCronExpression: Quartz cron expression. Example: '0 0 * ? * *' = every hour on the hour.
schedule._comment_pauseStatus: UNPAUSED = active schedule. PAUSED = defined but disabled.
